description_long: This is a two-tiered, corner-shaped wooden stool or small shelving unit, designed to fit snugly into a right-angled corner. The structure consists of two triangular shelves supported by four vertical wooden posts, with the rear post extending slightly above the top shelf for added stability. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the piece a natural, rustic appearance. The surfaces are flat and the edges are slightly beveled, with no visible decorative elements or hardware. The open sides and back allow for easy access to both shelves, making it suitable for use as a display or storage unit in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or public waiting areas. The overall construction appears sturdy, with a focus on functionality and simplicity.
description: A rustic, two-tiered wooden corner stool or shelf with open sides and visible wood grain, designed for functional use in urban spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triangular shelves and four supporting posts, with the rear post extending above the top shelf.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the shelves and the vertical alignment of the posts, with the rear post most prominent.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the open back and the connection points of the shelves to the posts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same vertical post structure and the open, accessible design of the shelves.
